entries.d: a TV show about animals and nature
entries.s: We watched an interesting _____ with real footage of wild lions hunting in Africa.

entries.d: a film with magic and unreal things
entries.s: The magical _____ had wizards casting spells, fire-breathing dragons, and a talking unicorn.

entries.d: a TV show with real people, not actors
entries.s: My sister loves watching that _____ with real people living together in a house every Friday.

entries.d: a story with magic and unreal things
entries.s: I am reading a _____ about a magic school for young wizards who ride broomsticks.

entries.d: a TV show where people answer questions
entries.s: He won 10,000 euros on the TV _____ by answering 20 general knowledge questions correctly.

entries.d: a film about the future or space
entries.s: The _____ was about a future year 3000 where robots took over Earth with laser guns.

entries.d: a programme that tells you what happened today
entries.s: My dad always watches _____ on TV at eight o'clock to hear about politics and the world.

entries.d: a story about solving a crime
entries.s: In the _____, the clever police inspector found fingerprints and solved who robbed the bank.

entries.d: a film about people falling in love
entries.s: My mum likes to watch a _____ with a girl meeting a boy and falling in love on Saturday evenings.

entries.d: a TV show about sport
entries.s: We watched the live football World Cup game between Germany and Brazil on the _____.

entries.d: a scary story
entries.s: The _____ with ghosts, vampires, and a haunted house was so scary that I could not sleep all night.

entries.d: a serious TV show with many episodes
entries.s: The new _____ on TV with eight episodes about a family hospital is really exciting.

entries.d: a story about exciting things happening
entries.s: The _____ was about a group of children searching for buried treasure on a tropical island.

entries.d: a short film made for a song
entries.s: Have you seen the new _____ with the singer dancing in a colourful background and her band?

entries.d: a story about people falling in love
entries.s: She wrote a _____ about a handsome prince who fell in love with a beautiful princess.

entries.d: a film or show with drawn characters
entries.s: My little brother watches a colourful animated _____ with talking animals every morning on TV.

entries.d: time you spend looking at a screen
entries.s: My parents say I have too much _____ on my phone and tablet and should go outside more.

entries.d: the title of a news story
entries.s: The big bold _____ at the top of the newspaper front page said: BIG STORM HITS CITY.

entries.d: the most new, the most recent
entries.s: Have you heard their _____ song that came out yesterday? It is their newest hit.

entries.d: a funny film or show that makes you laugh
entries.s: We watched a funny _____ and laughed out loud the whole time until our stomachs hurt.

entries.d: one part of a TV series
entries.s: I watched the first _____ of the new show and now I want to see all the other 9 episodes.

entries.d: a person who plays video games a lot
entries.s: My older brother is a passionate _____ and plays video games for hours every day after school.

entries.d: a type of, a sort of
entries.s: What _____ of music do you like — pop, rock, jazz, or classical?

entries.d: a little, more than a bit
entries.s: The maths test was _____ easy — a bit tricky but not very hard. I got most right.

entries.d: to watch a film or show on the internet
entries.s: We can _____ the new film tonight on our tablet on Netflix without downloading it.

entries.d: Saturday and Sunday
entries.s: What are you doing at the _____ on Saturday and Sunday when there is no school?

entries.d: to stop moving, to become very still
entries.s: When the music suddenly stopped in the game, everybody had to _____ and not move a single muscle.

entries.d: very, very big
entries.s: We saw a _____ blue whale — the biggest animal in the world, 30 metres long — in the deep ocean.

entries.d: in something, not outside
entries.s: It was pouring with rain outside, so we stayed and played board games _____ the warm house.

entries.d: to give money for something
entries.s: How much money did you _____ for that book at the shop? It looks expensive.

entries.d: to show where something is with your finger
entries.s: She raised her hand and used her finger to _____ the country Spain on the big wall map.

entries.d: a strong force or special ability
entries.s: The superhero had the magical _____ to become invisible and walk through solid walls.

entries.d: a small thing you use to control your TV
entries.s: I can not find the _____ for the TV. I want to change the channel to the news.

entries.d: to answer someone
entries.s: I sent her a text message this morning but she did not _____ all day. Maybe her phone is off.

entries.d: to give something for money
entries.s: They want to _____ their old car for 2000 euros and then buy a new one with the money.

entries.d: very, very small
entries.s: The baby bird was so _____ — only 2 centimetres long — that it fit in the palm of my hand.

entries.d: the sound you make when you talk or sing
entries.s: The female singer had a beautiful high _____ and everyone in the audience loved her.

entries.d: big from one side to the other
entries.s: The big brown river was very _____ — about 100 metres — and we could not swim across to the other side.

entries.d: to try hard against someone or something
entries.s: The two angry boys started to _____ with their fists over the last chocolate cookie on the plate.

entries.d: a person who has a shop
entries.s: The friendly _____ behind the counter helped me find the right book in her small bookshop.

entries.d: to go away so nobody can see you
entries.s: The magician said 'abracadabra' and made the white rabbit _____ from the top hat into thin air.

entries.d: to have something in your hand
entries.s: Can you _____ my heavy shopping bag in your hands for a moment, please? I need to tie my shoelace.

entries.d: to use time doing something
entries.s: We _____ every Saturday afternoon at our grandma's cosy house drinking tea and eating cake.

entries.d: to move the top of your body down
entries.s: She had to _____ from standing up to pick up the shiny gold coin from the floor.

entries.d: to put your arms around someone
entries.s: I always _____ my mum and wrap my arms around her when I get home from school.

entries.d: a big area of water with land around it
entries.s: We went swimming in the large clear _____ of still water surrounded by mountains on a hot summer day.

entries.d: a flat green thing that grows on a tree
entries.s: In autumn, every single green _____ on the maple tree turns yellow, orange, and red before falling off.

entries.d: to be flat, for example on a bed
entries.s: I like to _____ flat on the soft sofa and read a good book in the afternoon.

entries.d: the outside part of your body
entries.s: The sun was very hot and my _____ on my arms and face turned red and sore.

entries.d: a small round mark on something
entries.s: The ladybird has shiny red wings with small round black _____ — 7 dots on each side.

entries.d: not strong
entries.s: After being sick in bed with a fever for a whole week, I felt very tired and _____.

entries.d: not alive any more
entries.s: We found a _____ bird in the garden. It was not moving and its body was cold.

entries.d: how many fairy tales begin
entries.s: _____, in a land far away, there lived a beautiful princess in a big tall castle with a tower.

entries.d: at some time in the past or future
entries.s: _____, a very strange mysterious letter arrived at our front door in a black envelope.

entries.d: the outside part of a book
entries.s: The front _____ of this new fantasy book has a big colourful picture of a dragon breathing fire.

entries.d: when two people are friends
entries.s: Their long _____ started on the very first day of school in Year 1 and they are still friends today.

entries.d: a person who lives next to you
entries.s: Our _____ who lives right next door to us has a big brown dog that barks a lot at night.
